description: Apollo 16 Flight Journal - Day 8, Part 2: LM Liftoff and Rendezvous

tokens: There is no recorded communication between Mission Control and Ken Mattingly until175:44:23, after the LM Ascent Burn has been completed.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Thirty seconds, elevation rise rate coming up as predicted, 1,563 feet above the lunar surface.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Forty seconds, all systems looking good at this time.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Coming up now on 1 minute; velocity reading 366 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Ascent thrust looking stable, moving up.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Range to go to insertion 154 nautical miles.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: The velocity [is] now reading 1,299 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Altitude now 22,460 feet above the lunar surface.
Velocity now reading 15,096 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: AGS and PGNS tracking right together, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's Charlie Duke reporting both guidance systems functioning right together.
Three minutes, 25 seconds, 29 000 feet in altitude.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: 31,710 feet in altitude; range to go 131 nautical miles.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Velocity now reading at 2,450 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: 41,385 feet in altitude above the lunar surface.
Range to go now 110 nautical miles.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Velocity now reading 3,090 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: FIDO reports two minutes to go.
[A slightly late call by the PAO.]
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Velocity now reading 3,642 feet per second.
Altitude now 51,039 feet above the Lunar Surface.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Six minutes 10 seconds; altitude 55,085 feet above the lunar surface, range [now] to 48 nautical miles.
Velocity now reading 4,536 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: On time, minus 0.3, minus 1.0, and plus 1.7.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: A reminder to hit the Stop button, John.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I have the tweak for you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We copied the Ground Elapsed Time for tweak burn at 175 hours 54 minutes 5 seconds.
We now show an orbit of 42 nautical miles by 8.9 nautical miles.
Standing by now for the results of the tweak burn.
Ground monitoring reports the tweak burn is completed.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Orion, we'd like you to use the - B System, so open the Crossfeed and close the Main SOV on System A.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: for a P20 auto maneuver pitching up.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Will need a state vector for a - [garble] to be ready?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: How soon do you expect to have one?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: This is Apollo Control Houston at 175 hours, 57 minutes aboard Orion and the crew has programmed - computer programmed 20 punch tone [sic] and this the rendezvous navigation program.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: That - John Young speaking to Ken Mattingly, both in orbit.
Young reports Orion has [the] Command Module visually.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We're still working on it, Ken.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We got the AGS and Auto update, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We show the two spacecraft 126 nautical miles apart.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Okay, Ken, if you'll go to Accept, we'll send an up-link for you.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Boy, Jim, the sunset is spectacular.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Orion, there will be no PIPA update.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Orion, I've got about 2.2 volts on the signal - on the radar.
Can you give me a better reading?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And I have you visually opposite the bright star.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We need to try to reacquire.
I'm several miles off from you.
We'll reacquire on the A Channel.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: A discussion between the two spacecraft indicated a distance of 113 miles.
We show a closure rate now of 424 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: How about another range check when you get a chance to, please.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're at 106.5 miles on the tapemeter.
That could be a couple miles off, Ken.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: A Verb 83 says we're at 105.72 miles, with five marks in.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Jim, we got 5988 on the weight.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This is Apollo Control Houston at 176 hours 6 minutes ground elapsed time.
We show the two spacecraft at a distance of 103 nautical miles apart and a closure rate of 402 feet per second.[PAO is 11 minutes ahead of true GET].
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Do you have your tracking light on [garble]?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Orion, this is Houston with TPI solution.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Delta-Vx, plus 77.6; Delta-Vy, plus 3.8; Delta-Vz, plus 3.1; for a total of 77.7.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We now show the two spacecraft at a distance of 79 nautical miles apart and a closure rate of 330 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We're Verb 32, and we ought to have pretty good numbers.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Ah, this is a sweet flying machine, I'll tell you.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We'll read it down here, John.
We can't read you very well.
It's just excessive noise on the loop.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: About 10 minutes away now from Loss of Signal with Casper.
We show a range between the two spacecraft of 74 nautical miles.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We're looking at 78.0, plus 2.7, and minus 0.2 on the ...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're looking at 78.0, plus 2.7, a minus 0.2 on the PGNS.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We show a range of 65 nautical miles between the two spacecraft and a closure rate of 277 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: How do we look for an APS TPI?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Orion, you're Go for an APS TPI.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: All right, what's your estimate of the burn time?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Orion, are you requesting burn time?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Burn time for TPI should be about 2.5 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: With 21 marks and 17 minutes, you want to just keep marking as opposed to Verb 93ing, right?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The answer to that, Jim, is yes, I'm sure.
Just want to make sure somebody thunked [sic] about it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, you're coming up on two minutes to LOS, and you're looking good.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We show a range, now, of 52 nautical miles, a closure rate of 215 feet per second.
Less than two minutes, now, from Loss of Signal, as Orion and Casper's pass behind the Moon.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Orion, you could do your final comp at 10 instead of eight.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Orion, this is Casper on Victor Hotel.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Read you loud and clear, Ken.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, this stuff is working pretty good today, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Man, I could see that thing - light of yours at 70 miles on the telescope, even.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We've had Loss Of Signal, now, with both spacecraft - Orion now in hot pursuit of Casper as they both pass behind the Moon.
We heard at least half of the conversation as the two spacecraft were talking before we had LOS with Casper.
We're at 176 hours, 24 minutes Ground Elapsed Time; and this is Apollo Control, Houston.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Yeah, I could see you visually right after liftoff, when we were supposed to be like 150 miles away.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Jim, we're 3 miles out and closing.
We did a TPI of plus 78.0, plus 2.7, plus 0.7, and we burned two mid-courses of minus 0.3, minus 0.1, plus 0.9; plus 0.5, plus 0.2, plus 0.7, and we got a visual.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Guess we don't need to tell you, but this is a sweet machine.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Orion, You are just a little tiny black dot to the unaided eye.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I still have you at 28 feet per second on the [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I show you as a little black [garble]".
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: You need to go to your [garble] need to go to your [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I thought the idea was we'd take turns.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That's the nicest thing anyone's said.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You look a lot smaller in the daytime.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, well, wait until you get back around there and take a look.
We know they aren't out very far, but want you to look at the covers.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: It is looking more like an animated cartoon as we go along.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: LM transcript has Mattingly "[Laughter.] [Garble] these cartoons in the real world."
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay, I'm ready to go to attitude whenever you are.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: When you're station-keeping, let me know.
We have some words for you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: John, looking at the pictures of the liftoff, it appeared that something might have come loose - skin on the - the back of the vehicle so, for that reason, we want Ken to take some pictures of the LM, so we have a slight modification to the Flight Plan.
We'll be asking you to do a yaw 360 after Ken does his Verb 49 manoeuvre to the docking attitude.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Roger; I see - Yaw 360 after Ken does a Verb 49 to the docking attitude.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, right here I've got good lighting
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: There's a lot of noise so we can't hear you well- well, but ...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Stand by, I'm - let me get my camera [garble] [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Let me just recap here the procedure...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Ken, we want you to do the - Ken, do the Verb 49 maneuver to the SIM bay attitude per Flight Plan and then do the 360-degree roll and, of course, following that, you do the Verb 49 maneuver to the docking attitude.
Then I have a change for the Flight Plan whenever you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're in perfect position to take pictures of the LM right now.
All they've got to do is to pitch.
He prefers to go to the other sequence, is that correct?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: If you're in a position to take pictures of the LM we wanted the LM to do a 360-degree yaw, and you're to take pictures of the - the minus-Z portion of the ascent stage, using the same camera setting that you have on the DAC and the EL, except the focus on the EL should be changed to infinity.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, we'll just take you up on that, Hank, and take pictures of the - where's minus Z?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, it's the back side of the LM.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I'm with you and I'll turn the DAC on, and I'll a - fix the EL [garble] station-keeping now.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, Ken, if you observe anything there, will you please relay your observations?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: On lift-off, one of the MESA blankets flew out in front of the LM and hit the ground about 200 meters in front of the LM.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I understand a portion of the MESA blanket's still on the - on the front.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, that might have been what you saw.
It came pretty high in the air and it went straight out to the west.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, everything on the plus-Y side looks clean - just the surface is a little flaky with paint [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, on the - on the back side ...
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Orion, will you go Forward Omni?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Looks like some of the thermal blanket around the ECAs ["Descent Engine" in LM transcript.] on the back end there is pretty badly chewed up.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Some of the stuff is torn - a couple of panels are torn off.
And some of the stripping in between it is - it looks like it was struck by something, but it looks like all the mylar blankets underneath are still intact.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, we got some more stuff on the other side [garble]...
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, can you observe whether it's possible for sunlight to directly impinge on portions of the spacecraft equipment?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, sir; it's not possible from the back; I can't tell about the bottom; but, on the back side, the Mylar blankets are still intact - it's only that outer covering that's broken.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I - I tell you, this thing really flies beautifully.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Sure wish airplane flying was this easy.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: LM transcript attributes this to Ken Mattingly - more believable given his current manoeuvring.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay, 16; we assume you're pressing on with the Flight Plan?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, I'm ready to go to my attitude.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, John, are you ready for me to go to my attitude?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That's a - wait a second, until we get in position.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, I'm just going to do a little - it'll be a roll and [garble][garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Orion, will you go Normal Voice?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, it will be a half degree a second.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, you have station-keeping; I'm maneuvering.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Casper, we need Rate 2 on the BMAG.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, Charlie, have you got a Forward VHF there?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, the things I'd like for you to look at are - you remember on the aft shelf of the SIM bay, there's two booms back there.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The one on my - the most- plus-Y one is the Mass Spec - It's got a white cover and it - it's kind of a rectangular shape cover.
The one on the left or - it's really in about the center of the SIM bay - at the aft end and it has a silver cover and that's the Gamma Ray.
Would you take a look and see if either of the covers are not quite closed because we have indications in the spacecraft that they're not closed and indications on the ground that they are.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: [Young in LM transcript] Are you maneuvering, Ken?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This is one of the fastest maneuvers I've made in a long time.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Well, I'm not there yet, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I'll tell you when I get there.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Got about 20 degrees of pitch and about 30 degrees of roll.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Now then, I wait for you to take a look.
And then, after you do that, why, I will do a 360-degree rotation about my X-axis while you take pictures of the thermal coating.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Can you tell me which side of the LM SIM bay?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, it's a dark Inconel cover.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, can you - you don't see anything white sticking out from under it?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, because the inconel shield on the Mass Spec is - black.
Okay, do you see the Gamma Ray door?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Orion, you have 32 minutes until darkness.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: On the quad above the SIM bay - do you mean the A quad?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, good; can you get some pictures of those, too?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, and I'm ready to start my 360 roll when you get some pictures of those things.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, I'm going to roll left.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Apollo Control Houston [at] 177 hours, 37 minutes Ground Elapsed Time.
Ken Mattingly aboard Casper doing a 360 degree roll per the Flight Plan.
Orion and Casper now station keeping.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: You guys are pretty bad; there's even debris outside up here.
It was nice and clean before you came back.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, I'm getting ready to - to spin over to - to a new attitude.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: ...Very close to the attitude we came up in.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Yes, sir; I'm going to docking attitude.
And it - it's almost identical to our rendezvous attitude.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Well, I got 90 degrees of roll to go.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Well, I got about 10 degrees in pitch.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Casper wants the Logic Go and Omni.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, they gave you Go, Ken.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Casper, let's hold up on the Pyro A.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I thought you just gave me a Go; but I guess not.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Casper, let's take the Logics, Off, and we'll go through it again.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You're Go for Pyro Arm - I'm sorry, Logics, On.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You can go ahead and start your maneuver, I'll take station-keeping.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I'll tell you when we have capture.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: end of LM transcript for mission.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: I don't have any barber poles.
Took a couple of extra blurps to get you.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, it looks - looks pretty fair.
How about if I just retract you?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We were wondering what took you so long.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We - we were doing these fancy maneuvers up here.
Hardly anybody ever gets to do a 360-degree yaw on the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're about 28 minutes to LOS, and I have about five pages of time-line changes, whenever you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Orion, you can close the Crossfeed and open A Main SOV.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Casper, let's hold off on the P20.
We need good comm to get all these changes up.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Timeline book changes - start on page 13 and change 173:55 to 177:54.
And under "Configure S-band," Item 1, change CS - "CSM maneuver to jettison attitude" to "CSM maneuver to SIM bay minus-X forward attitude" and delete "Proceed with prep "et cetera.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Item 2, delete Item 3, change "Prim" to "Secondary" first line, change "Right" to "Off" and "Hi" to "Lo." Add a second line, "S-Band antenna, Aft"; and delete remainder of Step 3.
Add the following: "Exterior Light, Off; perform docked deactivation (staged), Steps 1 to 7 of the Contingency Checklist, page 3-9." Over.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That's the "Verify jet attitude." Step 3, sec - "S-Band, Secondary, Off, and Lo." Delete - mixed - all the steerable stuff, and add "External Light - Exterior Light, Off," which it is, and "Docked deactivation (staged), Steps 1 through 7" in the Contingency Book.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Next is change 1 - 174 hours to 177:59 under "Prep for transfer" and step 3, change line 4 to read "LMP purge valve (1, aft LHSSC)"
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That's - all I did was add in "LMP purge valve." All the - all the rest was there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I have a - a question here, Charlie, but then, on the next line, it's - under neck ring, they said add "Stow commander's purge valve" - oh, that's "Serial number 194 in ISA bottom pocket."
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, your purge valve apparently is serial number 197, and John's is 194.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, if you've got that, I'm doing a Step 5 - it's delete first two lines.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, we've been using the purge valves interchangeably.
I don't know who was using whose when we were out on the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we'll talk about that later.
Let me get the - the rest of the change to you.
Under Step 9, after "SE Audio, Open," add "ECS LCG Pump, Open." Delete remainder of step.
Insert the following: "Cabin Gas Return, Open; Suit Circuit Relief to Auto - that's a verify; Suit Isol Valve, both, to Suit Disconnect.
Both, doff suits - suits, install PGA electrical connector covers from the purse, and stow LCG plugs in suits from the purse.
Install neck ring dust covers in the purse.
Transfer suit to CMP." Then configure circuit breakers per 3-10 and 11 of Contingency Checklist.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: "Cabin Gas Return," add after "SE Audio, Open." "LCG, Open" - correction, "LCG Pump, Open; Cabin Gas to Open; Suit Circuit Relief to Auto; Suit Disconnect - Suit to Hose Disconnect; doff suits; put on electrical connectors, the LCG plugs, dust covers." And then go to 3-10 for the CBs on Contingency Checklist.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Then on page 14, step 11, delete "PPKs, three, on the left-hand midsection and the aft SRCs." That's in parentheses.
The next item is the flag kit.
Next item is flight data file.
Then add Timeline Book and LM Contingency Checklist.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Scratch PPKs, the flag kit, the flight data file, and add the Timeline Book and the Contingency Checklist.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And delete "MSFN up-link, update, target PGNS, and configure AGS, step 1." Over.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, then on page - we'll delete page 15, 16, and 17, and then on page 18, under "IVT to CSM," delete step 2, and prior to "LM to CM transfer list," add "Perform final deactivation per Contingency Checklist, Page 3" [garble].
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, hold on a minute - hold on a minute - hold on, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You're going faster than he can move.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I can't turn the pages that fast.
I'm on - I've deleted page 16 and 17, and I'm on page 18.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, under "IVT to CSM," delete step 2, and prior to "LM to CM transfer list," add "Perform final deactivation per Contingency Checklist, page 3-12 to 3-15." Over.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, on "IVT to CSM," delete step 2.
Perform final deactivation per 3-12 to 15 in Contingency Checklist.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, and then under the transfer list, delete "DSEA, PPKs, flag kits flight data file in the jettison bag, purse and contents, unused food, used urine bag, and used fecal bags." Over.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And then go back to Page 14, Charlie.
Let me know when you're there.
I think we've missed something there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, on the right-hand side of that page, we want to delete, essentially, three blocks there; the "MSFN up-link/update," all the steps under that - the five steps; then the next, "target PGNS"; and then "Configure AGS." All that portion is to be deleted.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, under "Configure AGS," all those AGS - the four AGS entries there are - are deleted.
We pick up there with Step 2.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, then the - the following are changes in the LM Contingency Checklist on page 3-9.
Step 4, add "Verify" - Do you want me to wait until you get to the Contingency Checklist, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You - you better wait until he floats over there to it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Shouldn't take us long to go through the Contingency Checklist changes ...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, on Page 3-9, Step 4, add "Verify" after "Master Arm Off." Then delete "Audio, Commander"; and Step 6, add "Verify" after "Mode Control, Both, Off." Then Step 7, delete first two lines.
And then on - why don't you repeat up to that point?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Step 4: "Verify Master Arm, Off"; delete "Audio, Commander; Mode Control, Both, Off, Verify." "Window Shades and Commander transfer," delete.
We'll just leave him over here, huh?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey; Jim, I - I - I guess I think that to verify the Master Arm, Off, is kind of a silly change to make Charlie copy, if you got 25 pages of that.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: No, listen, John; we've just about one more page, and we'll be finished.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, on Page 3-10, Secondary Power Amp, Close.
And then on Page 3-11 - let me know when you're there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, row 3, SE Audio, Open, and Primary Amp, Open.
And then we'll go to Page 3-12.
Let me know when you're there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Step 2, change "Audio, LMP" to "Audio, Both." Change line 2 to read "VHF B and VHF A Transmitter and Receiver, Off." Then Step 4, add, after "Secondary S-Band Transmitter and Receiver, Open," "Secondary Power Amp, Open, and delete "Primary Power Amp, Open." Then Step 5, delete three lines and add "Connect LM/CM umbilicals." Over.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: The last one was Step 5 to delete three lines and and "Connect LM/CM umbilicals." Over.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, then on step 6, under "To use CSM Power," and "LM power dash 1 and LM power dash 2, Main B, circuit breakers, Closed, and CSM- in parentheses." Over.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay then, Page 3-13, looks like we just have about three or four more changes here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, on 3-13 on - und - under row 3, Open Utility Light.
On row 5, open Translunar Bus Tie, and then the next change is on Page 3-14.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: On 3-14, row 2, Open ASA; row 4, Open S-Band Antenna and Translunar Bus Ties.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, next, and the last is on Page 3-15, Charlie.
Let me know when you're there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Step 9, add, after "Ascent Water, Close," "Cabin Gas Return to Egress," and last change is Step 9 - Delete the first line.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Just to power this moose down, and then come back in tomorrow and fire it up again for jettison?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's affirmative, Charlie, and will you get the AGS Mode Control, Off?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Orion, if you'd like, I'll go through the - the basic plan here, post-docking, so it's - be clear in your minds.
The first step, of course, is to doff suits in the LM, and, of course, we're going to postpone some of the LM transfer until after you wake up.
We're powering down the LM, and we are going to - we're drying out the water boiler.
And we'll be ready to close out the LM at - at 179:20.
That's AOS plus 10 minutes on this next pass.

And then Step 5 is - we'll - y'all will get in the LM tomorrow and complete the transfer and LM jettison, and you will need the LM Timeline Book and the LM Contingency Checklist at docking to accomplish the deactivation.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'll be with you in just a minute.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, I got the drogue out - I mean the probe and the hatch out and I'm ready for the drogue, so if you're going to do anything that affects the pressurization, let me know.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, that first part, "Docked deactivation stage," was that just Steps 1 through 7 or the whole thing?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, you'll have to open the door and find out.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, Casper, you can go into your P20 attitude, minus-X forward.
transcript.speaker: CC

tokens: John verifies that the Master Arm is Off.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're finished with that update, so you can press on with the minus-X forward.
transcript.speaker: CC

tokens: Okay, 16, we're about 30 seconds from LOS.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, when you see us next time, we'll be [garble].
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And AOS time, 179:08; and your angles are zero - zero and 170.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: This is Apollo Control Houston, at 178 hours, 23 minutes Ground Elapsed Time [178:12 actual GET].
We've just had Loss of Signal with Casper as the spacecraft passes behind the Moon on its 53rd revolution.
We'll take the line down at this time and bring it back up at that time when we reacquire.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Start of Lunar Rev 54 at about 178 46.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: We can just barely hear you.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Orion, we'd like for you to switch Omni antennas.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Ken, do you know whether John and Charlie still have their comm carriers on?
Are they monitoring comm with us?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And Charlie just said that he's got a [garble] bus off-scale low, but the battery looks okay.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, will you have them close the circuit breaker on Panel 11, Signal Conditioner 1, on row 3?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, is there something in particular that you want?
I'll see if I can get them for you.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're just interested in getting data from the LM to - so we can go ahead with the closeout.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: This is Apollo Control in Houston [at] 179 hours, 16 minutes Ground Elapsed Time.
We presently show Apollo 16 with an apolune of 65.5 nautical miles perilune of 54.3 nautical miles on the 54th revolution around the Moon.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We read you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Somebody wanted me up for comm for some data or something, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We just - they just wanted to look at the LM status.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Jim, I guess he's not copying you.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We were wondering, you know, whether you began the ...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We've completed the deactivation - dock to deactivation sta- stage, rather.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Can you confirm PCM - PCM Bit Rate, Lo?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Charlie, you did call Primary Evap Flow Number 1, Closed, I believe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I gave you a mark on that.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Verify that you have Ascent Water selected and verify Primary Evap Flow Number 2, Closed.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, that Primary Evap Flow 1 and 2 that just went Closed, I got the wrong valve.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Verify that you're in SIM Bay attitude.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: [Garble] in the proper RCS configuration for SIM bay operation?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Could you tell us where you are in the Timeline Book?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I'm putting the buddy - the BSLSS rock bag in the - Ken's stowage thing here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You didn't get down to Step 9 in the post-docking time line yet, did you?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And the - everything else has been done.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I gave you some changes there, which included configuring circuit breakers per the Contingency Checklist.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I was going to wait until I got my suit off.
You want me to do that now?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We know where you are now, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'd like to verify Mass Spec Experiment switch: On, and Ion Source to Standby.
We're not receiving any data down here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I thought that the Experiment was On but it looks like it's Off.
I don't know whether we didn't turn it on or it got knocked off.
But it's On now and Source is in Standby.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: It's going to take some load to dry out that water boiler.
And, we're thinking of bringing some more equipment back on to reduce that dry-out time.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Casper, will you go Auto on High Gain?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: A few things that we want you to activate here so put a load on that water boiler.
Have five items, if you're ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Why don't you just read them to me and I'll turn them on?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Suit Fan Number 1 circuit breaker on Panel 11, Closed.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, then on panel 16, Inverter Number 2, Closed, and ATCA, Closed.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston [at] 179 hours, 42 minutes Ground Elapsed Time.
That was Jim Irwin reading up procedures to Lunar Module Pilot Charles Duke for speeding the drying process of a water boiler aboard Orion.
The two spacecraft [are] now docked and in the 54th revolution around the Moon.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: If it's convenient, Charlie, will you put the Biomed into the Right position?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Sure looks like a duststorm in this cockpit right now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, Jim, all the gear has been transferred into [garble] and we're gonna - I'm getting out of my suit now.
We'll be off comm for about 10 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'd like you to advise John and Charlie that we'd like them to be on biomed tonight.
Tell them we'd just like to make sure that - get a comparison of their good performance on the surface.
See if it's the same in orbit.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Is Charlie gonna get back on comm again shortly in the LM?
If not, I have a short procedure here for you to give to him.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we want Mass Spec Ion Source, On, in Casper.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: [Garble] open those four circuit breakers which you've closed.
And I'll repeat them for you.
That's Suit Fan Number 1 on Panel 11, Inverter 1 on Panel 11, and then on Panel 16, Inverter 2 and - and ATCA.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And looks like the boiler's drying out.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'll advise you on that in a few moments.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Apollo Control, Houston [at] 180 hours, 9 minutes Ground Elapsed Time, Flight Director Gene Krantz now polling this flight control team for go for Lunar Module close out.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: You have a Go for closeout.
However, dry-out will not be complete until 180:30, at which time you can continue with your deactivation.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: What - what do you mean, a Go for closeout, then?
You want me to wait until the water boiler dries out before I do anything else?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Before you pull any more power off.
In other words, do your circuit breakers.
We want you to wait on that until 180:30, which is about 18 minutes from now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Only thing we got left over here is the suits, and we'll get them over now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And at that time, Charlie, you should be on Page 3-12 of the Contingency Checklist.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Give me that time again for water boiler dry-out.
And I'll start taking off the comm and stuff.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm gonna go off comm over here, then, and - but won't pull any more breakers until 30.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we're going LOS in about 10 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: This is Apollo Control [at] 180 hours, 21 minutes Ground Elapsed Time [180:10 actual GET].
We've had Loss of Signal as Apollo 16 went around the corner on Revolution 54.
The Gold Team of flight controllers taking over here in the control room.
As the White Team headed up by Gene Kranz prepares to leave, get some sleep because they have a quick turnaround and relieve the Gold Team in turnaround 8 o'clock later this morning.
There will not be a change of shift press briefing with the White Team.
44 minutes till next Acquisition of Signal.
Flight Plan showing rest period to begin around 181:30 about an hour and 10 [to] 12 minutes; an hour and 8 minutes from now.
At 180:22 [180:11 actual GET], this is Apollo Control.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: This is Apollo Control [at] 181 hours, 6 minutes [180:55 actual GET] into the mission of Apollo 16.
Less than a minute away from acquisition of the combined Apollo 16 spacecraft, Orion and Casper, docked after successful stay on the Moon, coming up on lunar orbit number 55.
Flight planners here in the Control Center are devising a revised Flight Plan for the period up to Trans-Earth Injection at which time we should be back on the pre-mission flight time.
This will take forcing of the Ground Elapsed Time, what they call a GET update of some 22 hours.
The revised flight plan should be completed by around 8:00 am this morning.
Standing by for first call to Apollo 16.
The Apollo 16 crew will be put to bed fairly early through this front side pass.
They've had a rather busy day today and will have an equally busy one tomorrow with sub-satellite jettison, Lunar Module jettison, and Trans Earth Injection, coming up in fairly rapid succession.
No word yet from the crew of Apollo 16.
Spacecraft communicator, Tony England, standing by for their call, checking with [the] Flight Director on some manoeuvre PADs, timelines to be read up to the crew.
The communications engineer reporting to flight that we may be slightly late in getting signal locked up because of the antenna position on the spacecraft.
Let's just stand by on air [to] ground until the first call is made.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We'd like you to go to Accept, and we'll st - send up a revision to your state vector, and we'll send up your jet monitor and activate it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That's the first we'd heard from you.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, we're having a little trouble with our comm link, here.
We'll be back to you in a minute.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And we have a TEI-60 PAD here, whenever you want to take it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Comments: GDC align, Sirius and Rigel; roll align, 131; pitch, 029; yaw, 016.
And the longitude at TIGis minus 171.96.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Sirius and Rigel are set stars; roll align, 131; pitch, 029; yaw, 016.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And we'd like you to go Accept, and we'll update your state vector and send up the jet monitor.
And we'll go ahead and activate it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Understand you'll update the state vector and activate this jet monitor when you get it set up.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Are you going to spend all day there, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Oh, my wife's gotten so tired of me being around the house, she just sent me out.
I might as well stay here.
Gee, I was in 2 hours today.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Gerry, here says he hasn't had a chance to congratulate you on an outstanding job up there, so the - this whole team's sending up a 'Well done'.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We really appreciate the stuff they've been doing for us, I'll tell you.
We're gonna have to get a thing going at the - at the place down there when we get back.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Everybody - everybody agreed with that in a here.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, John, could you go Accept, please?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Hey, Tony, now about a few words on what the general plan is for tomorrow?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; stand by one, and I'll get them ready.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: For tomorrow, we'll give you a Flight Plan update in the morning, but just a resumé here.
We'll have you wake up at 189:30.
That'll give you a good night's sleep, there.
And we'll transfer back over the LM and get the rest of the gear back over and activate the LM.
Come back to the Command Module and don the suits, and jettison the LM, and jettison the satellite.
And towards the end of the day, we'll do a TEI.
We've got a plan for using the Mapping Camera and Altimeter most of the - I think it's most of the day.
And we have some Pan Camera passes also.
We'll get the details on that up tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, we're not going home with any blank film, are we, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: All the Pan [Camera film] will be used up, but it looks like we'll have several hundred feet of the Mapping Camera [film].
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well, one thing about it, Tony with one suit on and one suit off a day, if - you don't have to worry about your exercise periods.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You're not going to wear out the ropes tomorrow, huh?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Doing the suit donning and doffing is the equivalent of wearing out a set of ropes.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You guys will be all set for a Houdini act.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Hey, Tony, did - Tony, did you say we get up at 8 - 189:30?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's what I was just looking at.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Don't you think you can sleep that long?
Golly, I'd think you'd sleep 12 hours.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And y'all have an estimate on when you'd be ready to go to bed?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Probably - I - we just started eating.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And you can go back to Block, and your EMP is running.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And our plan here is - once you get to bed, we'll do all we can to not disturb you until it's necessary to meet the schedule for tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: If we stay up all night, tomorrow morning we're gonna be awake at 189:30.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: There's some slop in that, John.
We may be able to even slide that some.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'd just as soon get up a couple of hours earlier and use the Mapping Camera film.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You're really socking it to us here
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I wonder if you could give us an estimate on how much time you're going to need in the LM tomorrow before you get to the LM activation.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Around 30 minutes would probably do it, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Incidentally, John, you got three dots from the Cape.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Apollo 16, somebody may be on VOX, there.
Every once in a while, we have you keying down here.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, Apollo 16, we were a little late on acquisition, there.
We'd like you to verify that your High Gains's set up on zero Pitch and Yaw 170.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We saw you with a low signal strength, and -
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, but we're - we're in Auto instead of React [sic].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Apollo 16, if you have a chance, there, we'd like an E-Mod.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, Apollo 16, we've got all we need for the night.
Why don't you press on through there and then, your pre-sleep, just record the read-outs.
Don't bother sending them down, and we won't bother you anymore.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: As the Sun sinks slowly in the west, we bid a fond farewell to all MCC.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Still about 30 minutes remaining on this front-side pass, [on] Revolution 55.
However, the crew of Apollo 16 has essentially signed off for the next 8 hours.
Apollo 16 now in an orbit measuring 54.1 by 65.4 nautical miles.
Cabin pressure aboard Casper 5.5 pounds per square inch.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: This is Apollo Control at 183 hours, 12 minutes Ground Elapsed Time into the mission of Apollo 16; now to start in the front-side pass on revolution number 56 in lunar orbit.
Orbit is now measuring 54.8 by 66.1 nautical miles.
Another hour and five minutes remaining on this front-side pass before the spacecraft goes around the corner to begin revolution 57.
Revolution count starts at 180 degrees longitude, which is directly opposite the Earth's side of the Moon.
Orbital weight at the present time is 43,856 pounds, a little more than 21 tons.
Six hours and 16 minutes remaining in the sleep period until the crew is awakened for a rather busy day.
Meanwhile, here at the Control Center, the Gold Team, those who aren't busy with planning for the subsequent days' activities, are watching a rerun of the EVA-3 video tape.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: This is Apollo Control [at] 186 hours, 12 minutes Ground Elapsed Time; some four minutes away from Loss Of Signal as the sleeping crew or Apollo 16 nears the end of their 57th lunar orbit.
That orbit at the present time measuring 54.5 by 66.2 nautical miles.
Cabin pressure during this pass holding 5.3 pounds per square inch at a temperature of 72 degrees.
All three crewmen apparently fairly sound asleep at this time.
They have some 3 hours, 16 minutes remaining before reveille call.

And then a rather busy day continuing to close out the Lunar Module, [and] prepare it for its de-orbit and impact manoeuvre, jettisoning the Ascent Stage, jettisoning of the sub-satellite to be left in lunar orbit and then the Trans Earth Injection burn late in the day, propelling the Command Service Module onto [an] Earthward track.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: This is Apollo Control; 187 hours, 12 minutes Ground Elapsed Time.
Two hours, 17 minutes remaining in the crew rest period.
Apollo 16 currently early in its 58th lunar revolution front-side pass around the Moon.
By medical data on the Command Module pilot Ken Mattingly and Lunar Module Pilot Charlie Duke being received, mean heart rates running in the 40's for both men.
Cabin temperature in the Command Module showing 68 degrees.
Cabin pressure, 5.2 pounds per square inch.
New Flight Plan update is out up through the end of Trans Earth Injection.
Flight Plan update had been published up through beyond Trans Earth Injection.
There will be a GET update, that is the Ground Elapsed Time will be forced to agree with [the] pre-flight plan.
At 191 hours Ground Elapsed Time will suddenly become 212 hours, 48 minutes Ground Elapsed Time, for a change of 21 hours, 48 minutes.
In other words, [time] during revolution 76 of the pre-flight plan will agree with the real time Revolution 65.
I trust everyone is sufficiently confused!
Trans-Earth Injection burn will be at 222 hours, 21 minutes under the new GET.
transcript.speaker: PAO
